content.problemStatement:
Chef's playlist contains 333 songs named A,BA, BA,B, and CCC, each of duration exactly XXX minutes. Chef generally plays these 333 songs in loop, i.e, A→B→C→A→B→C→A→…A \rightarrow B \rightarrow C \rightarrow A \rightarrow B \rightarrow C \rightarrow A \rightarrow \dotsA→B→C→A→B→C→A→…

Chef went on a train journey of NNN minutes and played his playlist on loop for the whole journey. How many times did he listen to the song CCC **completely**?


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases. The description of the test cases follows.
Each test case consists of a single line of input, containing two space-separated integers N,XN, XN,X.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the number of times Chef listens to the song CCC **completely**.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains two space‑separated integers N and X — the duration of Chef's train journey in minutes and the duration of each song in minutes respectively.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing the number of times Chef listens to the song C completely.

solution.approach:
Each song has duration X minutes and the playlist order is A → B → C, repeating forever. A full cycle therefore lasts 3*X minutes and contains exactly one complete listening of song C. The number of complete C songs Chef can hear in N minutes is simply the number of full cycles that fit into N, i.e., floor(N / (3*X)). This can be computed with integer division.
Algorithm per test case:
1. Read N and X.
2. Compute cycle = 3 * X (use 64‑bit integer to avoid overflow).
3. Answer = N / cycle (integer division).
4. Print answer.
The algorithm runs in O(1) time per test case and O(1) extra space.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – constant work for each of the T test cases.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few variables are used.
